description_long: This object is a pair of metal pliers with a robust, utilitarian design, commonly used for gripping, bending, or cutting wires and other materials. The handles are gently curved for ergonomic comfort and appear to be made entirely of metal, lacking any visible insulation or rubberized coating. The jaws are flat and broad, with a serrated gripping surface for enhanced traction on objects, and a squared-off tip that suggests suitability for gripping rather than precision work. The pivot joint is a classic fixed type, allowing the two arms to rotate around a single axis for a strong, stable grip. The overall finish is smooth and metallic, with a slightly reflective sheen, indicating a steel or similar alloy construction. No visible branding, logos, or decorative elements are present, emphasizing a purely functional aesthetic.
description: A sturdy, all-metal pair of pliers with curved handles and flat, serrated jaws, finished in a smooth metallic sheen. The design is utilitarian and unbranded, suitable for gripping and manipulating objects.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved handles and the flat, serrated jaws.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open, squared-off jaws and the curvature of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handles and the alignment of the jaws.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the jaws and the spread of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, all-metal handles with no insulation or ergonomic padding.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, broad jaws with serrated gripping surfaces and squared-off tips, optimized for general gripping tasks.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single-axis pivot joint, non-adjustable, providing stable movement.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal with no additional grip pattern or texturing on the handles.
